info.title: Kurator API
info.description:
REST API for Kurator: collections, items, search, external metadata lookup, session-based auth, bundled SQL migrations with first-boot bootstrap, and optional S3-backed image uploads.

**API v2:** **`/api/v2/hitlists`** adds permalink slugs, internet-**public** visibility, stub entries, votes, and comments (see tag hitlists-v2).

**Authentication:** Sessions are opaque tokens stored hashed in the database. The API accepts (1) the **kurator_session** HTTP-only cookie (typical browser flow via the Next.js same-origin proxy) or (2) **`Authorization: Bearer <session_token>`** on any protected route. When both are present, the **cookie wins**. After **POST /api/v1/auth/register**, **POST /api/v1/auth/login** (when 2FA is not required), or **POST /api/v1/auth/login/2fa**, the JSON body includes **session_token** — the same secret as the cookie — for native and non-cookie clients. **POST /api/v1/auth/logout** revokes the session for either mechanism; browsers also receive `Set-Cookie` clearing the cookie.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Registration, login, logout, password recovery, beta invite access. Session delivery: `Set-Cookie: kurator_session` plus JSON `session_token`. Authenticated API calls: cookie and/or `Authorization: Bearer <session_token>` (cookie preferred if both sent).

tags.name: notifications
tags.description: In-app notification feed and read state (requires session).

tags.name: shelf-share
tags.description: Shared shelves: join requests, invites, access request decisions.

tags.name: collections
tags.description: Collections (shelves); list visibility rules depend on session for some scopes.

tags.name: hitlists-v2
tags.description: Hitlists (v2): public permalinks, stubs, votes, comments. Base path **/api/v2/hitlists**.

SessionCookie.name: kurator_session
SessionCookie.description: HTTP-only session cookie set on successful register/login/2fa. Preferred when both cookie and Authorization Bearer are present.

BearerToken.name: Authorization
BearerToken.description: Bearer followed by the opaque session_token from POST /auth/login, /auth/login/2fa, or /auth/register (same secret as the kurator_session cookie). Example: Bearer <session_token>

get.description: Returns configured OAuth providers (google, discord) for the sign-in UI. Empty when credentials are not set.

Redirects the browser to Google or Discord. On success, **GET /auth/oauth/{provider}/callback** sets **kurator_session** and redirects to the web app **next** path (default /). When private beta is off, unknown accounts are registered and provisioned with a starter collection. When **BETA_ACCESS_REQUIRED** is on, OAuth is login-only for existing linked accounts; new sign-ups use email invite + **POST /auth/register**.
Exchanges the authorization code, creates or logs in the user, sets **kurator_session**, and redirects to the web app. Errors redirect to **/login?oauth_error=** with a machine-readable code.

302.description: Redirect to app or login with error

post.description: Creates the user, returns profile fields and **session_token**, and sets **kurator_session** (HTTP-only). Native clients should persist **session_token** and send **Authorization: Bearer** on subsequent requests.

201.description: Created; response extends User with session_token

post.description: If 2FA is required, returns **pending_token** (no session yet). Otherwise returns **user** and **session_token**, sets **kurator_session**, and native clients may use Bearer with **session_token**.

200.description: Either two_factor_required + pending_token, or two_factor_required false + user + session_token

post.description: Completes login after **POST /auth/login** returned **two_factor_required** and **pending_token**. Returns **user**, **session_token**, and sets **kurator_session**.
Returns **user**, **session_token**, and sets **kurator_session**. Passkey login skips TOTP even when 2FA is enabled.
Invalidates the session from **kurator_session** cookie or **Authorization: Bearer**. Clears the session cookie when present.

post.summary: Reactivate a deactivated account within the grace period

get.summary: Shared shelves for account deletion UI

delete.description: Invalidates all sessions. Optional JSON body lists voluntary ownership transfers for shared shelves.

post.summary: Accept shelf ownership after owner account deletion (sole collaborator)
Vote for new shelf owner after owner account deletion (election)

get.description: Requires Kurator Pro (**SessionCookie** or **BearerToken**). Returns saved YAML or defaults.

delete.description: Removes saved draft YAML. Fails with 409 if the theme is still published (unpublish first).

post.description: Removes all published marketplace versions. Clears active theme for the owner and affected Pro users; notifies users who were actively using the theme.

delete.description: Deletes the user's theme draft and own library entry. Requires the theme to be unpublished first.

get.summary: List Google Font families for theme editor autocomplete

get.description: Requires Kurator Pro. Returns own saved theme and marketplace installs.

patch.description: Pass library_id as UUID string or null to clear.

post.description: Requires **SessionCookie** or **BearerToken**. Returns a Stripe Checkout URL for Kurator Pro (monthly or annual).
Requires **SessionCookie** or **BearerToken**. Updates an active Pro subscription between monthly and annual Stripe prices (proration handled by Stripe).

409.description: Already on that interval or cannot switch

post.description: Requires **SessionCookie** or **BearerToken**. Returns a Stripe Customer Portal URL to manage an existing subscription.
Verifies Stripe-Signature using STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET. Handles checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.created/updated/deleted, invoice.paid, and invoice.payment_failed.

get.description: Requires **SessionCookie** or **BearerToken** (either satisfies security).
Lightweight poll endpoint for the activity bell badge. Does not return notification rows.

Redirects to Google or Discord to link the provider to the signed-in account. Provider email may differ from the Kurator account email. Completes at the shared OAuth callback; redirects to settings with oauth_linked or oauth_link_error.

post.description: When beta enforcement is enabled. Queues email and may notify admins.

get.description: HTML response; validates emailed admin token query param `t`.
Sets beta unlock cookie and redirects to registration.

post.description: Provide totp_code (2FA on) or email_code (2FA off). Revokes all sessions on success.

post.summary: Request to join a shared shelf
Invite users to a shared shelf
Approve a pending shelf access request
Dismiss a pending shelf access request

get.description: Recently updated collections, lists, and wishlists for the signed-in user.
userRef is numeric user id or username.
Without owner_user_id requires session (own lists). With owner_user_id, optional session for visibility.

schema.description: name, description, visibility, is_public, is_shared, invite_user_ids

get.description: Without owner_user_id requires session. With owner_user_id, optional session for visibility.
Optional session: when omitted, only wishlists with visibility public are returned.

schema.description: name, description, target_collection_id, visibility, cover_art_url, is_shared, invite_user_ids

get.description: Optional session: when omitted, only entries on public wishlists are returned.

purchase_url.description: Optional http(s) link where the item can be purchased (e.g. Amazon, eBay).
Optional http(s) link where the item can be purchased (e.g. Amazon, eBay).

patch.summary: Update wishlist entry purchase link only
patch.description: Sets or clears purchase_url on an existing entry without resubmitting title, category, or metadata.

purchase_url.description: Optional http(s) link where the item can be purchased; null or empty clears.

post.summary: Materialize wishlist entry as collection item

get.description: Optional session for visibility on private items.
Lists the viewer may see that reference this item.

get.summary: Discover hitlists or list by owner
get.description: Without **owner_user_id**: returns non-private hitlists visible to the caller (public lists for anonymous users; followers/friends rules when signed in). Query **sort** orders the discover feed. With **owner_user_id**, returns that owner’s lists visible to the viewer (same as profile).

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Discover ordering when owner_user_id is omitted (default recent).

post.description: Creates a list with optional slug, comments_enabled, and entries_numbered (default true). Visibility may include **public** (internet). Public hitlists require a unique slug.

get.summary: Get public or visible hitlist by permalink slug
get.description: Optional session for vote/follow context. Unauthenticated callers only resolve **public** hitlists.
Optional session. Unauthenticated callers only resolve **public** hitlists. Response includes vote_count and comments_enabled.

post.summary: Add item link or stub entry

put.summary: Reorder hitlist entries (owner or shared editor)
put.description: Body must list every entry id on the hitlist exactly once, in top-to-bottom display order.

patch.summary: Update hitlist entry description (per-row blurb; markdown)
patch.description: Sets optional **description** on a list entry (item link or stub). Send empty string to clear.

description.description: Markdown-supported blurb for this row only (max 4000 chars after trim)

200.description: OK — current vote total and whether the viewer has upvoted
OK — current vote total and whether the viewer has upvoted

post.summary: Check slug availability and suggest disambiguated slug

exclude_list_id.description: List UUID to exclude from slug collision checks

alternate.description: When true, suggest a memorable slug (name-word-surname) after the base slug was taken

onboarding_step.description: Current onboarding step (1–5); 0 treated as 1 for new accounts.

has_shelves.description: True when the account owns any collection, wishlist, or list (skips onboarding overlay).

subscription_status.description: Stripe subscription status when subscribed; empty otherwise.

subscription_interval.description: Pro billing cadence when subscribed; empty otherwise.

RegisterResponse.description: Public user profile fields returned on registration, plus session_token for Bearer auth.

session_token.description: Opaque session secret; send as Authorization: Bearer <session_token>. Same value as kurator_session cookie body.

LoginResponse.description: Password login result: either 2FA challenge or completed session.

pending_token.description: Present when two_factor_required is true; use with POST /auth/login/2fa

session_token.description: Present when two_factor_required is false; Bearer token and same as kurator_session cookie
Opaque session secret for Authorization: Bearer

slug.description: Hitlist permalink segment when present (list rows only).
Permalink segment when set (e.g. public hitlists).

onboarding_step.description: Target step after validating the current step (must be current+1).

onboarding_completed.description: Set true on step 5 to finish onboarding.

entries_numbered.description: When true (default), the UI shows ordered entry ranks; false is unordered presentation.

viewer_has_voted.description: Discover feed (authenticated): whether the viewer has upvoted this hitlist. Omitted when false/unauthenticated.

collection_id.description: Omitted when the item is standalone (not on any shelf).

owner_user_id.description: Set only for standalone items (no collection_id).

collection_id.description: Omit to use default shelf; explicit JSON null creates a standalone item (not on any shelf). Prefer also sending standalone_item true when creating a standalone item so missing or stripped null does not fall back to the default shelf.

standalone_item.description: When true on create, forces a standalone item (not on any shelf), same as collection_id: null. Use when intermediaries may strip JSON null or omit collection_id would otherwise select the default shelf.

may_edit_entries.description: True when the authenticated viewer may add or curate items (owner or shared collaborator); false for browse-only visibility.
